content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.5-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against b047db8533afb6c271bb089fbde06211ad7720b9 and the changed source/test files. `nix build` passed; a direct `cargo test --locked --features test-stub drain_harness_test` could not run because rustup has no default toolchain configured. Found one correctness gap in the harness ready ordering.

## Amendments

1. `src/test_support/drain_harness.rs:300` returns ready beads in raw `issues.jsonl` order. Because `append_follow_up_on_disk` writes follow-ups with priority 1 at `src/test_support/drain_harness.rs:383`, the new backend-exhausted test now expects the older P2 bead to land before the newly filed P1 follow-up (`tests/unit/drain_harness_test.rs:139`). Production `br ready` is priority-aware, and `drain_bead_queue` consumes the first ready bead, so this harness can still exercise a different queue order than the real drain. Sort the recomputed ready list to match the default `br ready` order, and update the test expectation so the P1 follow-up is selected before lower-priority existing work.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: The work item is specifically about mirroring production ready-bead behavior; preserving file order means the recovery scenario can still pass while disagreeing with the production queue selection that the harness is meant to model.
